question: Considering all races where pit stop data is available, and focusing on instances when a driver was not behind another car on the previous lap but is behind on the current lap (accounting for retirements, pit-stop entries, pit-stop exits, and race starts), how many times has each type of overtake occurred in Formula 1?
evidence: Overtake classifications: R (Retirement) - overtaken driver retired on same lap as overtake. P (Pit) - two scenarios: Pit Entry (overtake while overtaken driver entering pit lane, pitted same lap); Pit Exit (overtake as overtaken driver exiting pit lane, especially if pitted previous lap and time gap less than typical pit stop duration). S (Start) - overtake on first lap, drivers within two grid positions at start. T (Track) - default classification when none of above conditions apply.
